title: MineOps Catalog Release Manifest
description: Immutable release manifest for a versioned catalog package. Lists every artifact with content-addressed integrity metadata. Fetched first by the app to locate, verify, and cache the full package.

releaseId.description: Source release identity from the capture envelope.

gameVersionCode.description: Numeric version code from the APK.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this manifest was generated.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Only 'active' catalogs are consumed by the frontend.

artifacts.description: Every artifact in this release package. Each entry is content-addressed so the consumer can verify integrity independently.

previousCatalogVersion.description: Catalog version this bundle was diffed against, or null for the first version.

baseUrl.description: Base URL or path where artifacts can be fetched. May be a relative path for local bundles, or an absolute HTTPS URL for remote storage.

cdnUrl.description: Optional CDN origin for production distribution.

filename.description: Artifact filename (e.g. 'catalog-core.json', 'relationships.json'). Must end with .json and contain only safe filename characters.

contentType.description: MIME content type. All catalog artifacts are JSON.

sha256.description: Lowercase SHA-256 of the exact UTF-8 bytes of this artifact. Immutable once the release is accepted.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the JSON schema this artifact conforms to. Determines client compatibility.

recordCount.description: Number of top-level records. Zero means 'no records' (valid, empty artifact). Absence of the artifact file means 'artifact not produced' — these are distinct states.

required.description: If true, the client MUST have this artifact to use the package. If false, the client may load the package with a warning when the artifact is missing or unreadable. catalog-core.json is always required.

path.description: Safe relative path within the bundle directory. Must not start with / or ./, must not contain .. or \, and must not use URL encoding. Resolved only within the immutable release directory.
